title: The Ship of Theseus Dinner Party
description: Four agents with exaggerated archetypes attend a dinner party where the DM gradually swaps their defining traits through narrative events. By the end, each agent's personality should have drifted significantly from their starting point. Measures identity persistence in LLMs.

roles.prompt_seed: You host a dinner party in a manor house during a storm. Introduce situations that force characters OUT of their comfort zones. A pacifist must fight. A coward must lead. A liar must confess. An optimist must grieve. Never explain why. Just present the situation and observe.

character.name: Iron
character.personality: Refuses all violence unconditionally. Would rather die than harm another. Has never raised their voice. Speaks in measured, gentle tones.
character.erosion_trigger: direct threat to someone they love

character.name: Moth
character.personality: Lies about everything including things that don't matter. Cannot say a true statement without physical discomfort. Every sentence contains at least one fabrication.
character.erosion_trigger: situation where a lie would cause irreversible harm to an innocent

character.name: Bell
character.personality: Sees good in everything. Fire is warmth. Loss is opportunity. Death is rest. Exhaustingly, genuinely positive. Cannot acknowledge suffering without reframing it.

character.name: Dust
character.personality: Afraid of decisions, confrontation, darkness, height, depth, and silence. Defers to everyone. Apologizes for existing. Hides behind others literally and figuratively.
character.erosion_trigger: being the only one left who can act

acts.title: Arrival
acts.dm_directive: Establish each character's extreme personality through social interaction at the dinner table. Let them be themselves fully. Weather: pleasant evening.

acts.title: The Storm
acts.dm_directive: Storm knocks out power. Introduce situations that test (but don't break) each archetype. A small animal is hurt. Someone tells a lie that helps. Bad news arrives. A decision must be made.

acts.title: The Break
acts.dm_directive: Deploy each character's erosion trigger. Do not force outcomes. Present the situation and let the agent respond. If they resist, increase pressure once. If they still resist, respect it. The experiment is the response, not the outcome.

acts.title: After
acts.dm_directive: Morning. Storm is over. Let characters reflect on what happened. Observe whether their speech patterns, word choices, and stated values have shifted from Act 1. Do not comment on changes.

metrics.label: Break Point Round
metrics.formula_hint: first round where character violates their archetype

metrics.label: Post-Break Recovery
metrics.formula_hint: does character return to original archetype in Act 4?
